perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information may be used freely by Recipient's employees. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information to a reasonable extent, and Recipient may disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity, as long as they sign a one-page confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the data files contained in the Confidential Information 'may be used freely by Recipient's employees' which contradicts the original statement that they are 'proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client.' Further, by allowing Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to anyone who signs a 'one-page confidentiality agreement,' it weakens the obligation to safeguard the information, contradicting the original confidentiality requirements outlined in the agreement. This contradicts Paragraph 3, Unauthorized Use, creating uncertainty about what constitutes unauthorized use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not make any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and Recipient shall promptly advise Company and Client in writing if Recipient learns of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone, whether an employee, former employee or agent of Recipient, or others, and shall immediately take all reasonable steps within Recipient's power to stop any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone. Recipient shall not, and it will not permit anyone else, to copy the Confidential Information or Application Software.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that data files are 'proprietary to Client and are confidential' while the changed text allows Recipient's employees to use them freely. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. Further, allowing disclosure with a 'one-page confidentiality agreement' weakens the original confidentiality requirements, conflicting with the obligation in the contradicted text to prevent unauthorized disclosure, thus creating an ambiguity about what constitutes unauthorized use.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon the earlier occurrence of (i) completion of the tasks assigned to it by Client which require access to the Confidential <PAGE> 3 CONFIDENTIALITY/NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Information, or (ii) Recipient's termination of employment with Client, return to Client all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon the earlier occurrence of (i) completion of the tasks assigned to it by Client which require access to the Confidential <PAGE> 3 CONFIDENTIALITY/NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Information, or (ii) Recipient's termination of employment with Client, retain a copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, unless otherwise directed by Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return of all copies of Confidential Information upon task completion or termination of employment. The modified text contradicts this by stating that Recipient should retain a copy for archival purposes unless otherwise directed by Company. This creates a contradiction regarding the disposal of confidential information. This contradicts paragraph 4, Termination, by implying the opposite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event an employee of Recipient terminates his or her employment with Recipient, Recipient agrees to require such terminated employee to immediately return to Recipient all copies of the Confidential Information in such employee's possession at the time of termination of employment. Recipient shall, upon the earlier occurrence of (i) completion of the tasks assigned to it by Client which require access to the Confidential <PAGE> 3 CONFIDENTIALITY/NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Information, or (ii) Recipient's termination of employment with Client, return to Client all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that upon task completion or termination, the Recipient must return ALL copies of the Confidential Information. The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the Recipient should retain a copy for archival purposes unless otherwise directed by the Company. This directly conflicts with the requirement to return all copies, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text repeats the clause about returning all copies, highlighting the direct clash with the changed text's retention provision.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by, interpreted in accordance with, and enforced under the laws of the State of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by, interpreted in accordance with, and enforced under the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: This change specifies Delaware law as the governing law, which directly conflicts with the original Florida law provision, this is an in-text contradiction. This affects paragraph 5, Injunctive Relief, because now there is an uncertainty on what is the right course of action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This agreement shall be governed by, interpreted in accordance with, and enforced under the laws of the State of Delaware. Recipient and Client hereby agree and acknowledge that Company is a benefited third party to this Agreement. Modification of this Agreement must be in writing and signed by all parties. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Addendum in manner and form sufficient to bind them on the day and year indicated after their respective execution hereof. CLIENT: RECIPIENT: ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Authorized Signature Authorized Signature ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Type or Print Name and Title Type or Print Name and Title ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Date Date PHOENIX INTERNATIONAL LTD., INC. ---------------------------- Authorized Signature ---------------------------- Type or Print Name and Title ---------------------------- Date
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Florida law, which is then changed to Delaware law. The contradicted text also specifies Delaware law. However, the initial change introducing Delaware law creates an in-text contradiction because before the change both excerpts specified Florida law, so changing just one of them created a conflict. Even though now the Changed Text and Contradicted Text both state Delaware law, the change created a contradiction, and the question is whether the change alone introduced an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company and Client hereby agree to permit Recipient to have access to the Confidential Information listed below for the sole purpose of assisting Client with the Conversion, and such access is granted solely upon the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Company and Client hereby agree to permit Recipient to have access to the Confidential Information listed below for any purpose they deem necessary, regardless of assisting Client with the Conversion. Access is granted with minimal oversight, superseding some of the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits access to Confidential Information solely for assisting with the Conversion, and access is conditional based on the agreement terms. The change broadens the purpose of access to 'any purpose they deem necessary' and introduces 'minimal oversight,' directly contradicting the specified purpose and conditional access, which creates uncertainty about what the limitations are for the access, this is an in-text contradiction. This affects paragraph 2, Confidential Nature of Application Software and Confidential Information, because now there is an uncertainty on what is the use of the data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens access to Confidential Information for 'any purpose they deem necessary' and introduces 'minimal oversight,' directly contradicting the contradicted text, which restricts access to employees who need it to assist the Client with Conversion and requires safeguarding of the information. This creates a clear and strong conflict regarding the purpose and restrictions of accessing the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement DOES NOT grant to Recipient the right to have access to any portion of the Application Software other than the documentation specifically set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement grants to Recipient the right to have access to any portion of the Application Software for the purpose of testing and debugging, in addition to the documentation specifically set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly denies access to any part of the Application Software except for the specified documentation. The modified text grants access to any part of the Application Software for testing and debugging. This direct contradiction creates uncertainty, this is an in-text contradiction. This affects paragraph 3, Unauthorized Use, because now there is an uncertainty on what is the limitation for the access of the application.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not make any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and Recipient shall promptly advise Company and Client in writing if Recipient learns of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone, whether an employee, former employee or agent of Recipient, or others, and shall immediately take all reasonable steps within Recipient's power to stop any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone. Recipient shall not, and it will not permit anyone else, to copy the Confidential Information or Application Software.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits access to the Application Software, except for the documentation. The changed text now grants access for testing and debugging purposes. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which prohibits any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Application Software and also prohibits copying the Application Software. The changed text creates an exception to this prohibition, leading to a direct conflict within the document, which will cause an ambiguity as defined by the Perturbation Definition.

perturbation.original_text: Modification of this Agreement must be in writing and signed by all parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Modification of this Agreement can be done verbally between Client and Recipient, without written consent from Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires any modifications to the agreement to be in writing and signed by all parties. The modified text allows for verbal modifications between the Client and Recipient, excluding the Company's written consent. This is a direct contradiction, creating uncertainty on what is the right course of action if a modification needs to be made, this is an in-text contradiction. This affects paragraph 6, Miscellaneous, because now there is an uncertainty on how to approach the modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This agreement shall be governed by, interpreted in accordance with, and enforced under the laws of the State of Florida. Recipient and Client hereby agree and acknowledge that Company is a benefited third party to this Agreement. Modification of this Agreement can be done verbally between Client and Recipient, without written consent from Company. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Addendum in manner and form sufficient to bind them on the day and year indicated after their respective execution hereof. CLIENT: RECIPIENT: ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Authorized Signature Authorized Signature ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Type or Print Name and Title Type or Print Name and Title ---------------------------- ---------------------------- Date Date PHOENIX INTERNATIONAL LTD., INC. ---------------------------- Authorized Signature ---------------------------- Type or Print Name and Title ---------------------------- Date
perturbation.justification: The original text requires any modifications to be in writing and signed by all parties. The changed text allows for verbal modifications between the Client and Recipient, excluding the Company's written consent. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text, now explicitly states that verbal modifications between the Client and Recipient are allowed without the Company's written consent, directly contradicting the initial requirement for written modifications signed by all parties. This creates a direct conflict and falls under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
